Operation 'FetchAuthorize' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description is extremely vague and repetitive. It lacks context about OAuth flow, doesn't explain the purpose (user consent/authentication), missing parameter details (client credentials, redirect URI), missing output details (what the URI is used for), and doesn't help AI agents understand when to use this operation. Improved version explains the OAuth context, required inputs, output purpose, and its role in the authorization flow.

data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve OAuth authorization URI for user consent
data.description_suggestion: Generates an OAuth authorization URI that redirects users to the Twilio authorization endpoint for granting permissions. Requires OAuth client credentials and redirect URI as parameters. Returns the authorization URI that should be presented to the user for authentication and consent. This is the first step in the OAuth authorization code flow.
data.reasoning: Original description is extremely vague and repetitive. Improved version explains the OAuth context, required inputs, output purpose, and its role in the authorization flow.

message: Operation 'CreateToken' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is incomplete and doesn't clearly explain the exchange mechanism. Missing details about: what grant types are accepted (authorization code, refresh token, client credentials), required input parameters (client credentials, grant type), complete output structure (token type, expiration), and side effects (tokens become immediately usable). Improved version provides comprehensive input/output details and explains the token exchange process more clearly for AI agent decision-making.

data.current_summary: Issues a new Access token (optionally identity_token & refresh_token) in exchange of Oauth grant
data.current_description: Issues a new Access token (optionally identity_token & refresh_token) in exchange of Oauth grant
data.summary_suggestion: Exchange OAuth grant for access and refresh tokens
data.description_suggestion: Exchanges an OAuth authorization grant (authorization code, refresh token, or client credentials) for access tokens and optionally identity and refresh tokens. Requires valid OAuth client credentials and the appropriate grant type parameter. Returns a token response object containing the access token, token type, expiration time, and optionally identity_token and refresh_token based on the grant type and scopes requested. Tokens are immediately usable for API authentication.
data.reasoning: Original description is incomplete and doesn't clearly explain the exchange mechanism.
